subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: governance/rights/politics rebuilt from independent sources (IMF, World Bank, WHO, V-Dem, RSF, Freedom House, HRW, Amnesty, CPJ, TI); Azerbaijani state/state-aligned media EXCLUDED there, with economic figures admitted only via IMF/rating-agency validation and promotional framing discarded.
A dynastic authoritarian petrostate with strong external buffers but an intensifying 2024-26 rights crackdown and a fragile post-Karabakh 'negative peace'.
Standout drags: dynastic autocracy, near-worst press freedom, a resource-curse oil model, and the 2023 displacement of ethnic Armenians from Karabakh.

economic.notes: Strong oil-funded buffers and low inflation, against a declining-output resource-curse model and a de-facto peg.

fiscal_state.notes: Low government debt (~21-23% GDP) and large combined reserves/sovereign-fund buffers, but pro-cyclical and oil-dependent. (IMF 2026 Art IV)

monetary_independence.notes: A de-facto USD peg since 2017, with the central bank subordinating policy to oil/FX management. (IMF; S&P)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The manat is not a reserve currency. (definitional)

inflation.notes: ~5-6% and within the target band. (IMF 2026 Art IV)

banking_stability.notes: Low NPLs and recent investment-grade upgrades, against state influence and a 2015 devaluation/bank-crisis legacy. (IMF; Fitch/Moody's)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: A key non-Russian EU gas supplier but with declining oil output and a classic resource curse. (IMF; EU Council)

institutional.notes: A dynastic autocracy with no judicial independence and among the world's worst press repression.

rule_of_law.notes: A dynastic autocracy with no judicial independence, sham trials, and systemic corruption. (Freedom House; HRW 2026)

statistical_integrity.notes: Economic data is IMF-validated; political/governance data is opaque. (IMF; V-Dem)

civil_service_capacity.notes: Oil-funded delivery capacity but clientelist and patronage-driven. (TI; HRW)

press_freedom.notes: RSF ~167/180, with ~25 journalists jailed, major outlets shuttered, and a 2025 media-law tightening. (RSF; CPJ; Amnesty)

political_social.notes: Entrenched suppressed stability and a severe rights crackdown, with the Karabakh displacement a recent fracture.

political_stability.notes: Entrenched with no real opposition and a post-Karabakh nationalist boost - suppressed/managed stability, brittle long-term. (Freedom House; HRW)

civil_liberties.notes: Hundreds detained, with a 2023-26 crackdown intensifying. (HRW 2026; Amnesty; Freedom House)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: Limited legal routes, not a relocation destination, with an authoritarian ceiling. (Freedom House)

social_cohesion.notes: Managed, with the 2023 displacement of ~100k ethnic Armenians from Karabakh. (Freedom House; HRW)

demographics.notes: A relatively young, stable population structure. (World Bank)

geopolitical.notes: A transactional hedger reliable as a gas supplier, with a fragile post-Karabakh peace.

alliance_reliability.notes: A transactional hedger - Turkey-aligned, Russia/West-balancing, an EU gas partner - reliable as a supplier, not as a values-ally. (CGEP; EU Council)

conflict_involvement.notes: Won the 2023 Karabakh takeover; a 2025 framework is a 'negative peace' rather than a treaty, with residual war risk. (ICTJ)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: Not sanctioned (a US review act pending), with a managed capital account. (Congress.gov; IMF)

physical_practical.notes: Oil-funded Baku modernisation, against Caspian decline and seismic exposure.

climate.notes: The Caspian fell sharply over five years (hitting ports and oil terminals), with water stress and heat. (Nature Communications Earth & Environment)

healthcare.notes: Oil-funded Baku modernisation but uneven rural access. (WHO; World Bank)

infrastructure.notes: Modern in Baku and oil-funded, but uneven nationally. (World Bank; IMF)

crime_safety.notes: Low-to-moderate street crime, with a police-state caveat. (Numbeo - soft source)

disaster_insurance.notes: Baku seismic and flood exposure against low insurance penetration. (seismic-hazard literature)

summary: A dynastic authoritarian petrostate with genuinely strong external buffers - low debt, large oil-funded reserves, low inflation, and recent investment-grade upgrades - and a useful role as a non-Russian gas supplier to Europe. But it is run as a hardening autocracy: no judicial independence, sham trials, among the world's worst press repression with journalists jailed, and an intensifying 2024-26 rights crackdown. Its trade base is a declining-output resource curse, the 2023 takeover of Karabakh displaced ~100,000 ethnic Armenians, and the subsequent peace is fragile. Caspian decline and seismic exposure weigh on the physical side.
